purpose: The master agent's protocol for using the Project Knowledge Index (PKI) at {project_root}/.synapse/knowledge/ during the planning phase. Defines the 6-step pre-planning flow, manifest extraction, file ranking, annotation reading, knowledge block construction, and decomposition signals. Includes graceful-degradation rules so the master never blocks on a missing or corrupt PKI. Loaded during planning when PKI annotations are available.
gotchas: The PKI NEVER blocks planning — every step has a fallback. Missing manifest, malformed JSON, empty PKI, no matches all proceed with standard CLAUDE.md-only planning.

Never read the annotations directory directly — always go through manifest.json indexes (domain_index, tag_index, concept_map). Globbing annotations/ is slow, unstructured, and misses file-to-annotation mapping.
Cap annotation reads at 8-10 files — beyond this the planning phase takes too long and extracted knowledge exceeds prompt budget.
Stale annotations (stale: true) are still readable but MUST be marked with a staleness caveat in worker prompts. NEVER treat as authoritative.
Stale exports/imports may be wrong — do NOT use stale export/import data for task decomposition; have the worker read the file fresh.
Filter PKI knowledge PER-WORKER based on that worker's specific files — never inject the same PKI block into every worker prompt (causes context bloat and irrelevant knowledge).
PKI knowledge block budget: max ~100 lines (half of CONVENTIONS section's ~200 line budget). Per-file extraction max ~10 lines.
Gotchas are NEVER cut for budget — they prevent errors. Cut conventions first, then relationships, then patterns. Gotchas always stay.
PKI knowledge supplements, never replaces, CLAUDE.md — the convention map from CLAUDE.md is primary; PKI provides file-specific knowledge CLAUDE.md cannot capture.
If >50% of matched files are stale, log warn and suggest the user run !learn_update.
patterns: 6-step pre-planning flow: (1) check manifest exists -> (2) extract domains/tags/concepts from prompt -> (3) look up files via reverse indexes -> (4) read annotations for matched files (cap 8-10) -> (5) build PKI knowledge block -> (6) inject into worker prompts and inform decomposition.
Extraction priority per annotation: gotchas (critical) -> patterns -> conventions -> exports -> imports_from -> relationships -> purpose.
Knowledge block injection point: appended to CONVENTIONS section after CLAUDE.md-derived conventions, under [PKI] label.
Ranking signals: complexity:'high' (prioritize), stale:true (deprioritize), domains array length, appears in multiple indexes.

Decomposition signals: complexity:'high' files get dedicated tasks, 3+ gotchas need narrow scope, serves/consumes relationships imply same wave or explicit dependency, stale files need fresh reads in READ list.
conventions: PKI knowledge block format: GOTCHAS / PATTERNS / CONVENTIONS / STALE sections with [file_path] prefix per entry.
Skip PKI when: manifest doesn't exist, empty PKI, no domains/tags match, all matched annotations stale, pure CREATE task with no existing files, or CLAUDE.md alone fills the 200-line budget.
Log every PKI decision: matched domains/tags, file count, annotation reads, staleness warnings — makes augmented planning auditable.
Broad prompts -> match domains; narrow prompts -> match specific tags; cross-cutting prompts -> start with concept_map.
